fact: The collision occurred in dense fog off Nantucket
source: Alive on the Andrea Doria! The Greatest Sea Rescue in History, Pierette Domenica Simpson (2006); also widely documented

fact: The Stockholm had radar, and the officer misread or misinterpreted the radar information
source: Out of the Fog: The Sinking of the Andrea Doria, Algot Mattsson (2003); corroborated by official investigations

fact: The Stockholm's ice-hardened bow penetrated the Andrea Doria's starboard side
source: Collision Course: The Classic Story of the Collision of the Andrea Doria and the Stockholm, Alvin Moscow (1959/2004)

fact: The Andrea Doria listed so severely that lifeboats on one side could not be launched
source: Collision Course, Alvin Moscow (1959); the ship listed approximately 18° to starboard almost immediately, rendering port-side lifeboats unusable

fact: Forty-six people died in the disaster
source: National Geographic / widely documented: 46 deaths total (42 on Andrea Doria, 5 on Stockholm)

fact: Both ships turned toward each other, worsening the collision course
source: Collision Course, Alvin Moscow (1959); the conflicting radar interpretations led to converging course changes
